name: Facebook Ads
description: Track Facebook paid social campaigns using consistent UTM values for ads, placements, creatives, and audiences.
mistake: Mixing facebook, fb, Meta, and Facebook Ads as separate source values.
bestPractice: Use facebook as the source and paid_social as the medium for paid campaigns.

name: Instagram
description: Track Instagram bio links, stories, reels, creator posts, and ads with clean social campaign naming.
mistake: Combining Instagram and Facebook traffic under one vague source.
bestPractice: Separate instagram from facebook when reporting needs platform-level clarity.

name: YouTube
description: Track YouTube descriptions, pinned comments, shorts, sponsorships, and channel links in GA4.
mistake: Using video, youtube, and social inconsistently as the medium.
bestPractice: Use youtube as source and video as medium for channel and video placements.

name: LinkedIn
description: Track LinkedIn organic posts, sponsored content, newsletters, and lead generation campaigns.
mistake: Not separating employee advocacy from sponsored campaigns.
bestPractice: Use content values to separate sponsored posts, employee links, and newsletter placements.

name: TikTok
description: Track TikTok organic videos, paid ads, creator links, and shop campaigns with UTM parameters.
mistake: Using creator names as campaign names instead of source or content values.
bestPractice: Use campaign for the initiative and content for creator or placement variants.

name: Google Ads
description: Track Google Ads landing URLs for search, display, remarketing, and Performance Max reporting.
mistake: Mixing paid search, display, and remarketing into vague campaign names.
bestPractice: Use google as source and cpc as medium, then distinguish campaign types clearly.

name: Email Marketing
description: Track newsletters, lifecycle campaigns, automations, and promotional sends with reliable UTM values.
mistake: Using email, newsletter, and mailer interchangeably as the source and medium.
bestPractice: Use email as medium and reserve source for newsletter, lifecycle, or tool-specific origins.

name: Shopify
description: Track ecommerce campaigns that drive traffic to Shopify product, collection, and landing pages.
mistake: Using product names as sources instead of campaign or content values.
bestPractice: Track product or collection detail in campaign or content fields, not source.

name: WordPress
description: Track WordPress blog promotions, lead magnets, newsletter links, and partner campaigns.
mistake: Adding UTMs to internal WordPress navigation and overwriting acquisition data.
bestPractice: Use UTMs on external campaign links, not internal site navigation.

name: Next.js
description: Track campaigns that promote Next.js apps, SaaS landing pages, launch pages, and documentation.
mistake: Not preserving UTMs through redirects or middleware.
bestPractice: Test final landing URLs to confirm redirects keep campaign parameters.

name: Pinterest
description: Track Pinterest pins, boards, product pins, and visual discovery campaigns.
mistake: Treating Pinterest traffic as generic referral traffic.
bestPractice: Use pinterest as source and content values for pin formats or board placements.

name: X / Twitter
description: Track posts, threads, ads, profile links, and creator campaigns from X or Twitter.
mistake: Switching between x and twitter without a reporting standard.
bestPractice: Choose one source naming standard and document it before launch.

name: Reddit
description: Track Reddit posts, ads, community promotions, AMAs, and sponsored discussions.
mistake: Using subreddit names as medium values.
bestPractice: Use reddit as source and content for subreddit or placement details.

name: Quora
description: Track Quora answers, ads, promoted questions, and educational content campaigns.
mistake: Forgetting to distinguish organic answers from paid promotion.
bestPractice: Use content to identify answer, ad, or question placements.

name: Affiliate Marketing
description: Track affiliate partners, creator links, coupons, and referral placements with structured UTMs.
mistake: Using the same source for every affiliate partner.
bestPractice: Use source for partner identity and campaign for the promotion.

name: Influencer Campaigns
description: Track influencer bios, stories, videos, posts, newsletters, and event activations.
mistake: Combining all creators under one influencer source.
bestPractice: Use source for creator identity and content for placement or asset type.

name: Podcasts
description: Track podcast show notes, sponsorship links, host-read campaigns, and episode pages.
mistake: Not separating episodes or hosts in content values.
bestPractice: Use content for episode, host, or placement details.

name: Webinars
description: Track webinar registration links across email, social, partners, paid ads, and follow-up sequences.
mistake: Using webinar as both source and campaign for every event.
bestPractice: Use campaign for the specific webinar and content for CTA variations.

name: SMS Marketing
description: Track SMS promotions, alerts, reminders, and ecommerce offers with short UTM structures.
mistake: Using long unreadable campaign names in mobile links.
bestPractice: Keep SMS UTM values short, lowercase, and easy to scan.

name: QR Codes
description: Track offline QR scans from flyers, packaging, events, posters, menus, and business cards.
mistake: Using one QR URL for every print placement.
bestPractice: Use content values to separate flyer, poster, packaging, or booth placements.
